message:
feat(core,abi): port gai_strerror + hstrerror message lookups to core::resolv (bd-msgs-epic)

Lifts two small constant-table message lookups out of the abi:
gai_strerror (resolv_abi.rs, ~12 lines, 8 EAI_* codes) and
hstrerror_message_ptr (unistd_abi.rs, ~8 lines, 4 H_ERR_* codes).
Both are integer→message-string mappings for legacy resolver
errno-like reporting paths.

New core surface (in resolv::messages):
  pub const EAI_{BADFLAGS,NONAME,AGAIN,FAIL,FAMILY,SOCKTYPE,SERVICE,OVERFLOW}
  pub const H_ERR_{HOST_NOT_FOUND,TRY_AGAIN,NO_RECOVERY,NO_DATA}
  pub fn gai_strerror_text(errcode: i32) -> &'static str
  pub fn hstrerror_text(err: i32) -> &'static str

Constants match glibc <netdb.h> values; tests pin them so they
cannot drift silently. Both functions return canonical glibc
descriptions for known codes and the appropriate fallback string
("Unknown getaddrinfo error" / "Resolver internal error") for any
other value. Notable: hstrerror(0) returns the internal-error
fallback (not "Success"), matching historical glibc behavior.

abi changes:
  - gai_strerror shim: looks up the &str via core, maps it back to
    a NUL-terminated `c"..."` literal at the FFI boundary so the
    returned *const c_char is program-lifetime static.
  - hstrerror_message_ptr: same shim pattern.
  - Drop 4 H_ERR_* local constants from unistd_abi.rs (now in core).

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-msgs-2):
  - 7 new core::resolv::messages tests pass
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - 5/5 gai_strerror integration tests pass
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-msgs-epic.
